steps.title: Infinitely many solutions means identical lines
steps.content: When a system has infinitely many solutions, the two equations represent the same line. One equation must be a scalar multiple of the other. Find which answer choice is a multiple of the given equation.

steps.title: Simplify the given equation
steps.content:
Divide $-15x + 25y = 65$ by 5 (the GCD of 15, 25, and 65):

$$-3x + 5y = 13$$

Now check which answer choice simplifies to this same form.

steps.title: Simplify each choice and find the match
steps.content:
• A: $12x + 20y = 52$ → divide by 4 → $3x + 5y = 13$ (wrong—the $x$ coefficient should be negative)
  • B: $12x + 20y = -52$ → divide by 4 → $3x + 5y = -13$ (wrong—signs and constant don't match)
  • C: $-12x + 20y = 52$ → divide by 4 → $-3x + 5y = 13$ (correct—exactly matches)
  • D: $-12x + 20y = -52$ → divide by 4 → $-3x + 5y = -13$ (wrong—the constant should be positive)

That matches choice C — C is correct.
